questions.questionText: The primary mechanism for removing the alpha-amino group from amino acids is:
questions.explanation: Transaminases (aminotransferases) transfer the amino group to alpha-ketoglutarate, forming glutamate.

questions.questionText: Transamination reactions require which vitamin derivative as a coenzyme?
questions.explanation: PLP is the essential prosthetic group for all aminotransferases.

questions.questionText: The urea cycle takes place exclusively in the:
questions.explanation: The complete set of urea cycle enzymes is found only in hepatocytes.

questions.questionText: The rate-limiting enzyme of the urea cycle is:
questions.explanation: CPS I is allosterically activated by N-acetylglutamate (NAG).

questions.questionText: Which pair of amino acids is exclusively ketogenic?
questions.explanation: Leucine and Lysine cannot be converted to glucose; they yield Acetyl-CoA/Acetoacetate.

questions.questionText: Phenylketonuria (PKU) is most commonly caused by a deficiency in:
questions.explanation: PAH deficiency prevents conversion of phenylalanine to tyrosine.

questions.questionText: Alkaptonuria is characterized by dark urine and involves a deficiency in:
questions.explanation: Homogentisic acid accumulates, oxidizes, and polymerizes to form a black pigment.

questions.questionText: Serotonin is synthesized from the amino acid:
questions.explanation: Tryptophan is hydroxylated and then decarboxylated to form serotonin (5-HT).

questions.questionText: Nitric oxide (NO) is synthesized in endothelial cells from which amino acid?
questions.explanation: Nitric Oxide Synthase (NOS) oxidizes arginine to NO and citrulline.

questions.questionText: Maple syrup urine disease (MSUD) involves defective metabolism of:
questions.explanation: A defect in branched-chain alpha-keto acid dehydrogenase complex causes MSUD.

questions.questionText: In Amino Acid Metabolism, which statement is most accurate regarding the core definition and diagnostic concept?
questions.optionA: It reflects the most exam-relevant definition used in standard Biochemistry texts
questions.optionB: It is an obsolete definition no longer used in clinical practice
questions.optionC: It is used only in pediatric population and never in adults
questions.optionD: It has no diagnostic significance in NEET-PG pattern questions
questions.explanation: This tests foundational definition-level knowledge from standard Biochemistry references for Amino Acid Metabolism. Subject: Biochemistry. Chapter: Amino Acid Metabolism.

questions.questionText: The most important etiology/risk factor to suspect first in a typical Amino Acid Metabolism scenario is:
questions.optionA: The common high-yield risk factor repeatedly asked in entrance exams
questions.optionB: A rare cause seen only in isolated case reports
questions.optionD: An etiology excluded by routine first-line workup
questions.explanation: NEET-PG and INI-CET focus on common, high-yield causes before rare differentials. Subject: Biochemistry. Chapter: Amino Acid Metabolism.
